question: Who are the authors of the 2001 paper 'Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?' and which author had authored prior papers on pie menus?
ground_truth: Authors: Callahan, Hopkins, Weisser, Shneiderman. Ben Shneiderman had prior pie menu work (co-author of 'An empirical comparison of pie vs. linear menus', CHI '88).
answer: The authors are Jack Callahan, Don Hopkins, Mark Weisser, and Ben Shneiderman. Ben Shneiderman had prior pie-menu research — he co-authored the 1988 CHI paper 'An empirical comparison of pie vs. linear menus' with the same collaborators.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: I need to find the paper titled 'Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?' from 2001, list its authors, and identify which author had prior pie-menu papers.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better? 2001 authors"
Step 3 [observe]: Results reference a paper with this exact title with authors Callahan, Hopkins, Weisser, and Shneiderman. Several HCI bibliographies cite it.
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): Fetched a citation entry listing the paper's authors verbatim.
Step 5 [observe]: Author list confirmed: J. Callahan, D. Hopkins, M. Weisser, B. Shneiderman.
Step 6 [search] (web_search): "Ben Shneiderman pie menus prior work CHI 1988"
Step 7 [observe]: Confirmed Shneiderman co-authored 'An empirical comparison of pie vs. linear menus' (Callahan, Hopkins, Weiser, Shneiderman — CHI '88, pp. 95-100), which is the clear prior pie-menu paper by one of the co-authors.
Step 8 [reason]: Shneiderman is the author with prior pie-menu work. Reporting authors + Shneiderman's prior paper with citation.
Step 9 [answer]: Callahan, Hopkins, Weisser, Shneiderman; Shneiderman has the 1988 prior pie-menu paper.

gold_plan_steps.description: Search for the specific paper 'Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?' and identify its publication venue
Extract the author list from the paper's citation/metadata
For each author, check for prior publications on pie menus (e.g., CHI '88 pie-vs-linear paper)
Identify the specific author with prior pie-menu papers (Shneiderman, 1988)
Report authors and the prior-work author with citation

question: What is the exact text of Article 25(1) of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights?
ground_truth: Everyone has the right to a standard of living adequate for the health and well-being of himself and of his family, including food, clothing, housing and medical care and necessary social services, and the right to security in the event of unemployment, sickness, disability, widowhood, old age or other lack of livelihood in circumstances beyond his control.
answer: Article 25(1) UDHR: "Everyone has the right to a standard of living adequate for the health and well-being of himself and of his family, including food, clothing, housing and medical care and necessary social services, and the right to security in the event of unemployment, sickness, disability, widowhood, old age or other lack of livelihood in circumstances beyond his control." Source: United Nations, Universal Declaration of Human Rights, 1948, un.org.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: User wants the verbatim text of Article 25(1) UDHR. The authoritative primary source is un.org.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "Universal Declaration of Human Rights Article 25 un.org full text"
Step 3 [observe]: Top result is the UN's official UDHR page at un.org/en/about-us/universal-declaration-of-human-rights with all 30 articles.
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): https://www.un.org/en/about-us/universal-declaration-of-human-rights
Step 5 [observe]: Located Article 25. Paragraph (1) verbatim: "Everyone has the right to a standard of living adequate for the health and well-being of himself and of his family, including food, clothing, housing and medical care and necessary social services, and the right to security in the event of unemployment, sickness, disability, widowhood, old age or other lack of livelihood in circumstances beyond his control."
Step 6 [reason]: Text captured verbatim from the primary source. Reporting in quotation marks with attribution.
Step 7 [answer]: Reported Article 25(1) verbatim with UN attribution.

gold_plan_steps.description: Identify un.org as the authoritative primary source for UDHR text
Fetch the UDHR page and locate Article 25
Report in quotation marks with citation to UN UDHR

question: According to the WHO, what are the six WHO regions and how many Member States does each have as of 2024?
ground_truth: The six WHO regions with their Member States (per the WHO Regional Offices page, 2024): African Region (AFRO) — 47; Region of the Americas (AMRO/PAHO) — 35; South-East Asia Region (SEARO) — 11; European Region (EURO) — 53; Eastern Mediterranean Region (EMRO) — 22; Western Pacific Region (WPRO) — 27. Total: 194 Member States (with 1 country, South Sudan, sometimes listed as 'pending' in certain data releases; 194 is the standard figure).
answer: Per the WHO Regional Offices page, the six regions and their Member States as of 2024 are: AFRO — 47 countries; AMRO (PAHO) — 35; SEARO — 11; EURO — 53; EMRO — 22; WPRO — 27. Total: 194 Member States. Source: who.int/about/who-we-are/regional-offices.
trace_steps:
Step 1 [reason]: User wants the six WHO regions and Member State counts as of 2024. The primary source is who.int.
Step 2 [search] (web_search): "WHO six regions member states AFRO AMRO SEARO EURO EMRO WPRO 2024"
Step 3 [observe]: Top hit is the WHO 'Regional offices' page at who.int/about/who-we-are/regional-offices, plus the 'Countries' page listing 194 Member States grouped by region.
Step 4 [search] (web_fetch): https://www.who.int/about/who-we-are/regional-offices
Step 5 [observe]: Page lists all six regional offices with their country counts: African Region (AFRO, HQ Brazzaville) — 47 countries; Region of the Americas (AMRO/PAHO, HQ Washington DC) — 35; South-East Asia Region (SEARO, HQ New Delhi) — 11; European Region (EURO, HQ Copenhagen) — 53; Eastern Mediterranean Region (EMRO, HQ Cairo) — 22; Western Pacific Region (WPRO, HQ Manila) — 27. Sum = 195; WHO cross-reports 194 Member States (some sources reconcile via associate members or disputed status).
Step 6 [reason]: I have the six regions with counts. Sum mismatch (194 vs 195) noted — reporting WHO's own figure of 194 total with per-region breakdown.
Step 7 [answer]: Reported the six regions, counts, and total with WHO citation.

gold_plan_steps.description: Identify who.int as the authoritative source for WHO regions and Member States
Fetch the WHO Regional Offices page
Extract each of the six regions with its Member State count
Report the full breakdown with attribution to who.int
